metadata.summary: VLM fundamentals: single-image analysis, multi-image comparison, structured JSON extraction via Pydantic, and stateful visual Q&A with input methods, token cost control, and failure modes.

content.title: Vision Basics
content.summary: Analyze images with VLM APIs (GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet, Gemini Flash): single-image analysis, multi-image comparison, structured JSON extraction via Pydantic, and stateful visual Q&A. Covers input methods (URL vs. base64), detail settings, token cost control, and the failure modes that make VLMs unsuitable for precision tasks.

why.p: VLM API calls fail silently when images exceed provider size caps, base64-encode unexpectedly large requests, or produce unvalidated JSON that breaks downstream agents. Resize images to 1024px before sending (50-70% token reduction), enforce structured output via response_format, and cache by SHA-256 of image bytes to avoid re-scanning identical images.

list.item: Agents need to read content from screenshots, diagrams, or scanned forms.
Pipeline receives images as intermediate outputs (web scraping returns screenshot; agent reads the page).
Generating alt-text or captions for images at scale.
Classifying images (safe/unsafe, relevant/irrelevant) as a routing step.
Extracting text from images when dedicated OCR is unavailable or layout is complex.
Real-time video analysis at >2 FPS — VLM API latency (500ms-2s) is too high; use YOLOv11 or GroundingDINO locally.
High-volume barcode/QR decoding — use zxing or python-qrcode; 100x cheaper and deterministic.
Pixel-level measurement — VLM outputs are statistical estimates, not precise values.
Privacy-sensitive images that must not leave the local environment — use Qwen2.5-VL or LLaVA via Ollama.
Tasks reducible to image metadata (EXIF, creation date) — VLMs cannot see metadata; use Pillow or ExifTool.
Medical, legal, or forensic images where model content policies may silently reject inputs.

rule.statement: Send images as base64 for sensitive content. URL-based images are fetched by the provider's servers and may be logged or cached externally.
rule.rationale: Providers that accept URLs retrieve the image from an external server that they control. Base64 encodes the image inline in the request, keeping it within the API call itself.

rule.statement: Resize images to 1024px on the long edge before sending. This reduces token cost by 50-70% with minimal quality loss for text, diagram, and screenshot tasks.
rule.rationale: VLMs charge by image tokens, which scale with resolution. At 1024px, most documents and screenshots remain fully legible. Going higher rarely improves extraction accuracy.

rule.statement: Check image file size before making API calls: Anthropic limit is 5MB per image, OpenAI is 20MB. Exceeding these limits causes silent or unhelpful errors if not pre-checked.
rule.rationale: Provider size caps are per-image, not per-request. A 10-image request with 4.9MB images each can still fail if the provider enforces a total payload cap.

example.title: Resize and encode helper
example.code:
from PIL import Image import base64, io
def prepare_image(path: str, max_px: int = 1024) -> tuple[str, str]: """Resize to max_px on long edge, return (base64_data, media_type).""" img = Image.open(path) img.thumbnail((max_px, max_px), Image.LANCZOS) buf = io.BytesIO() fmt = img.format or "PNG" img.save(buf, format=fmt) data = base64.standard_b64encode(buf.getvalue()).decode() mt = {"JPEG": "image/jpeg", "PNG": "image/png", "WEBP": "image/webp", "GIF": "image/gif"}.get(fmt, "image/png") return data, mt
example.note: Prevents oversized requests that fail silently at the provider.

rule.statement: Label each image explicitly in multi-image prompts: "Image 1:", "Image 2:". VLMs lose track of which image they are describing without explicit labels.
rule.rationale: Without labels, VLMs conflate images or describe them out of order in comparisons. Explicit numbering anchors each reference in the model's response.

rule.statement: Always enforce structured output via response_format={"type": "json_object"} when downstream agents consume vision results. Prompt-only schema requests drift under real production load.
rule.rationale: VLM JSON output without format enforcement occasionally adds prose before the JSON, uses wrong field names, or omits required fields. response_format eliminates these cases for OpenAI; Anthropic requires explicit JSON-in-prompt instruction and parse-with-retry.

rule.statement: Wrap JSON parse in try/except and re-prompt with the specific parse error on failure. Do not silently drop failed extractions.
rule.rationale: Even with response_format, edge cases can produce malformed JSON. A re-prompt with the error message typically produces valid JSON on the second attempt.

example.title: Pydantic schema with retry pattern

rule.statement: Include the image only in the first message of a multi-turn visual Q&A conversation. Subsequent turns are text-only with conversation history.
rule.rationale: Including the image in every turn re-consumes image tokens on each message, multiplying cost by the number of turns. The model retains image context from the first message.

rule.statement: Always cross-validate numbers extracted from charts with a second VLM pass or a deterministic chart parser. VLMs frequently hallucinate chart values.
rule.rationale: Chart values are interpolated, not read precisely. VLMs produce confident-sounding but inaccurate numbers for bar heights, axis values, and table cells in charts.

rule.statement: Use detail="low" for quick image classification; use detail="high" for layout-heavy documents. Mismatching costs 4x tokens unnecessarily.
rule.rationale: OpenAI's detail setting controls how many 512px tiles are processed. "low" uses a single fixed tile (85 tokens); "high" tiles the full image (up to ~1700 tokens for a 1024px image). Using "high" for a simple yes/no classification quadruples cost with no accuracy benefit.

rule.statement: Cache extraction results keyed on SHA-256 of the image bytes, not the filename. Re-scanning the same image should never re-call the API.
rule.rationale: Filenames are reused across pipeline runs. A content hash is stable regardless of how the file is named or moved.

rule.statement: VLMs cannot read image metadata (EXIF, PDF author, creation date). Extract metadata with Pillow or PyMuPDF in a separate step; do not ask the VLM for it.
rule.rationale: VLMs see only the rendered pixel content of an image. EXIF data, file creation timestamps, and PDF document properties are invisible to them.

rule.statement: Treat handwritten text extraction as low-confidence and always require human review. Printed text below ~100px height also degrades significantly.
rule.rationale: VLMs are not fine-tuned as OCR engines. Handwriting accuracy varies widely; small printed text below 100px can be misread character by character.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A vision agent receives an image (URL or base64) and a task description, calls the appropriate VLM (Claude Sonnet for complex layouts, Gemini Flash for high volume, GPT-4o for general QA), and returns a structured Pydantic object. Upstream, a routing subagent classifies the image type (document, photo, screenshot, chart) and selects the VLM and prompt variant. For multi-image workflows, a fan-out pattern works well: spawn parallel vision subagents per image, then merge results in a reduction step.

example.title: Minimal Claude vision extraction
example.code:
import anthropic, base64, json from pydantic import BaseModel
class ImageResult(BaseModel): description: str text_content: str | None confidence: str # "high" | "low"
def extract_image(image_path: str, task: str) -> ImageResult: client = anthropic.Anthropic() with open(image_path, "rb") as f: data = base64.standard_b64encode(f.read()).decode() resp = client.messages.create( model="claude-sonnet-4-5", max_tokens=1024, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": [ {"type": "image", "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": "image/png", "data": data}}, {"type": "text", "text": f"{task}\nReturn JSON: {{description, text_content, confidence}}"}, ]}], ) return ImageResult(**json.loads(resp.content[0].text))

example.title: Prompt pattern for structured extraction
example.code:
Analyze this image and return JSON matching the schema below. - If a field is not visible or ambiguous, set it to null. - Do not infer values not present in the image. - Set confidence to "low" if any field required significant interpretation.
Schema: {schema_json}

section.name: subagent-roles
section.p: vision-router: classifies image type (document/photo/chart/screenshot) and selects VLM + detail setting. vision-extractor: calls VLM API, enforces structured output schema (Pydantic + response_format), returns typed result. vision-qa: stateful Q&A over a single image across multiple questions (maintains conversation history with image in first turn only). vision-validator: second-pass VLM call to verify specific extracted fields against the source image.

list.item: anthropic — Claude Vision API (Sonnet, Haiku). Install: pip install anthropic. Docs: docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/vision
openai — GPT-4o Vision API. Install: pip install openai. Docs: platform.openai.com/docs/guides/vision
google-generativeai — Gemini Vision API. Install: pip install google-generativeai. Docs: ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-understanding
Pillow — Image preprocessing: resize, compress, convert before API call. Install: pip install Pillow
pdf2image — Convert PDF pages to images for vision pipeline ingestion. Install: pip install pdf2image
pytesseract — Local OCR fallback for printed text (Tesseract). Install: pip install pytesseract. Docs: github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract

section.name: services-and-platforms
section.p: Anthropic API (SaaS, best for complex layouts and long-context documents). OpenAI API (SaaS, best structured JSON extraction via response_format; 20MB image limit). Google AI Studio / Vertex AI (SaaS, Gemini Flash for high-volume batch; Gemini Pro for multimodal reasoning). Replicate (SaaS, host Qwen2.5-VL, LLaVA, and other open-source VLMs with REST API). Ollama (OSS, run LLaVA, Qwen2.5-VL locally; no data leaves machine; use via REST). AWS Bedrock (SaaS, Claude + Titan on-prem-ish; enterprise compliance). Roboflow (SaaS, fine-tuned detection models, not general VLM; good for classification tasks).

rule.statement: Resize images to 1024px long edge before sending to reduce token cost by 50–70% with minimal quality loss for text/diagram tasks.
rule.rationale: Token reduction directly lowers API costs and latency.

rule.statement: Send images as base64 for sensitive content; URL-based images are fetched by the provider's servers and may be logged.
rule.rationale: Base64 keeps image data within the request body, avoiding external fetches.

rule.statement: Use GPT-4o detail: "low" for quick classification; detail: "high" for layout-heavy documents — mismatching costs 4x tokens.
rule.rationale: Detail setting directly controls token consumption and cost.

rule.statement: For stateful visual Q&A, include the image only in the first message of the conversation; subsequent turns are text-only.
rule.rationale: Reduces image token re-consumption and cost per turn.

rule.statement: Cache extraction results keyed on a SHA-256 of the image bytes — re-scanning the same image should not re-call the API.
rule.rationale: Prevents redundant API calls and reduces costs.

rule.statement: For batch classification, use structured output (Pydantic + response_format) so downstream agents always receive typed results.
rule.rationale: Type-safe downstream processing prevents silent failures.

antipattern.description: VLMs cannot read file metadata (EXIF, PDF author, creation date).
antipattern.reason: If your agent needs metadata, extract it with Pillow or PyMuPDF in a separate step before or after the VLM call.

antipattern.description: JSON schema compliance via prompt alone is unreliable.
antipattern.reason: Always parse with try/except and re-prompt with the specific parse error if validation fails.

antipattern.description: Image size limits are per-image, not per-request.
antipattern.reason: A request with 10 images at 4.9MB each can still fail if the provider enforces a total payload cap.

antipattern.description: Base64 encoding of a 5MB JPEG becomes ~7MB of text — request size bloats.
antipattern.reason: Monitor request size to avoid payload cap failures.

antipattern.description: Anthropic API image size cap is 5MB (not 20MB like OpenAI).
antipattern.reason: Pre-check before routing to Claude models.

antipattern.description: For financial or legal document extraction, not requiring human review on low confidence.
antipattern.reason: Require human review when confidence is "low" or extracted amounts exceed a business threshold before writing to any system of record.

antipattern.description: When comparing multiple images (e.g., before/after, product variants), not labeling them explicitly.
antipattern.reason: Label each image explicitly in the prompt ("Image 1:", "Image 2:") — VLMs lose track without explicit labels.

template.purpose: analyze_image_url, analyze_local_image, structured_analysis, VisualQA with Pydantic
Resize + base64 encode helper (1024px cap, returns media_type)
Agent prompt for structured vision extraction with null-on-ambiguity rule
